---
title: "Untitled"
author: "OMKAR"
date: "2025-11-06"
output: word_document
---


```{r}

#for T=1 week

library(DoE.base)
library(ggplot2)
# Create the data (without d1, d2, Exp_No)
So <- c(945, 945, 945, 964, 964, 964, 983, 983, 983)
K <- c(940, 965, 990, 940, 965, 990, 940, 965, 990)
r <- c(0.045, 0.065, 0.075, 0.065, 0.075, 0.045, 0.075, 0.045, 0.065)
sigma <- c(0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.25, 0.15, 0.2, 0.2, 0.25, 0.15)
BS <- c(466.1991753, 459.0597436, 454.8062531, 479.4440902, 
        473.7639579, 466.4841851, 495.7438806, 487.1454002, 480.7385638)

# Combine into dataframe
data <- data.frame(So, K, r, sigma, BS)
print(data)

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Calculate Signal-to-Noise Ratio (Larger-the-better)
# Because higher BS value = better option value
# Formula: S/N = -10 * log10(mean(1 / y^2))
#-----------------------------------------------------------

data$SN_ratio <- -10 * log10(1 / (data$BS^2))
print(data)

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Analyze mean S/N ratios for each factor
#-----------------------------------------------------------

So_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$So), FUN = mean)
colnames(So_SN) <- c("So", "Mean_SN")

K_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$K), FUN = mean)
colnames(K_SN) <- c("K", "Mean_SN")

r_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$r), FUN = mean)
colnames(r_SN) <- c("r", "Mean_SN")

sigma_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$sigma), FUN = mean)
colnames(sigma_SN) <- c("sigma", "Mean_SN")

print(So_SN)
print(K_SN)
print(r_SN)
print(sigma_SN)

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Plot main effects for each parameter
#-----------------------------------------------------------

# So
plot(1:3, So_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "red",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Stock Price (So)", ylab = "S/N Ratio",
     main = "Main Effect - So")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = So_SN$So)

# K
plot(1:3, K_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "green",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Strike Price (K)", ylab = "S/N Ratio",
     main = "Main Effect - K")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = K_SN$K)

# r
plot(1:3, r_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Interest Rate (r)", ylab = "S/N Ratio",
     main = "Main Effect - r")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = r_SN$r)

# sigma
plot(1:3, sigma_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "purple",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Volatility (σ)", ylab = "S/N Ratio",
     main = "Main Effect - σ")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = sigma_SN$sigma)

#-----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Identify best combination (highest S/N ratio)
#-----------------------------------------------------------

best_So <- So_SN$So[which.max(So_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_K <- K_SN$K[which.max(K_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_r <- r_SN$r[which.max(r_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_sigma <- sigma_SN$sigma[which.max(sigma_SN$Mean_SN)]

cat("Best combination:\n")
cat("So =", best_So, "\n")
cat("K =", best_K, "\n")
cat("r =", best_r, "\n")
cat("sigma =", best_sigma, "\n")

# Interpretation:
# The combination with the **highest S/N ratio** gives the 
# most stable and optimal (largest) Black–Scholes option value.




#for T=1 month


# ---------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Input Data (updated values)
# ---------------------------------------------
So <- c(945, 945, 945, 964, 964, 964, 983, 983, 983)
K <- c(940, 965, 990, 940, 965, 990, 940, 965, 990)
r <- c(0.045, 0.065, 0.075, 0.065, 0.075, 0.045, 0.075, 0.045, 0.065)
sigma <- c(0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.25, 0.15, 0.2, 0.2, 0.25, 0.15)
BS <- c(443.4281521, 430.0838869, 421.8755921, 461.3756042, 
        450.5763513, 434.0991302, 484.6512162, 466.0220256, 454.1069938)

# Combine into dataframe
data <- data.frame(So, K, r, sigma, BS)
print(data)

# ---------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Calculate Signal-to-Noise Ratio (Larger-the-better)
# Formula: S/N = -10 * log10(mean(1 / y^2))
# ---------------------------------------------
data$SN_ratio <- -10 * log10(1 / (data$BS^2))
print(data)

# ---------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Compute mean S/N ratio for each factor
# ---------------------------------------------
So_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$So), FUN = mean)
colnames(So_SN) <- c("So", "Mean_SN")

K_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$K), FUN = mean)
colnames(K_SN) <- c("K", "Mean_SN")

r_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$r), FUN = mean)
colnames(r_SN) <- c("r", "Mean_SN")

sigma_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$sigma), FUN = mean)
colnames(sigma_SN) <- c("sigma", "Mean_SN")

print(So_SN)
print(K_SN)
print(r_SN)
print(sigma_SN)

# ---------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Plot Main Effects for S/N Ratio
# ---------------------------------------------
# So
plot(1:3, So_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "red",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Stock Price (So)", ylab = "S/N Ratio (dB)",
     main = "Main Effect - So")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = So_SN$So)

# K
plot(1:3, K_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "green",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Strike Price (K)", ylab = "S/N Ratio (dB)",
     main = "Main Effect - K")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = K_SN$K)

# r
plot(1:3, r_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Interest Rate (r)", ylab = "S/N Ratio (dB)",
     main = "Main Effect - r")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = r_SN$r)

# sigma
plot(1:3, sigma_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "purple",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Volatility (σ)", ylab = "S/N Ratio (dB)",
     main = "Main Effect - σ")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = sigma_SN$sigma)

# ---------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Identify Best Combination (highest mean S/N)
# ---------------------------------------------
best_So <- So_SN$So[which.max(So_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_K <- K_SN$K[which.max(K_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_r <- r_SN$r[which.max(r_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_sigma <- sigma_SN$sigma[which.max(sigma_SN$Mean_SN)]

cat("Best combination based on highest S/N ratio:\n")
cat("So =", best_So, "\n")
cat("K =", best_K, "\n")
cat("r =", best_r, "\n")
cat("sigma =", best_sigma, "\n")

# ---------------------------------------------
# Step 6: Interpretation Guide
# ---------------------------------------------
cat("\nInterpretation Notes:\n")
cat("- Higher S/N ratio means higher and more consistent BS values.\n")
cat("- The level of each factor with the highest mean S/N gives the best robustness.\n")
cat("- The steepest line in the main effect plots indicates the most influential factor.\n")





#for T=3 months

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# TAGUCHI S/N RATIO ANALYSIS FOR BLACK–SCHOLES MODEL (T = 3 Months)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Input data
So <- c(945, 945, 945, 964, 964, 964, 983, 983, 983)
K <- c(940, 965, 990, 940, 965, 990, 940, 965, 990)
r <- c(0.045, 0.065, 0.075, 0.065, 0.075, 0.045, 0.075, 0.045, 0.065)
sigma <- c(0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.25, 0.15, 0.2, 0.2, 0.25, 0.15)
BS <- c(386.5884927, 369.8371938, 358.8392006, 413.0568184,
        398.0145278, 366.8620277, 444.0287332, 411.3118271, 395.5709381)

# Combine into dataframe
data <- data.frame(So, K, r, sigma, BS)
print(data)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Calculate Signal-to-Noise Ratio (Larger-the-better)
# Formula: S/N = -10 * log10(mean(1 / y^2))
# ------------------------------------------------------------
data$SN_ratio <- -10 * log10(1 / (data$BS^2))
print(data)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Compute mean S/N ratio for each factor level
# ------------------------------------------------------------
So_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$So), FUN = mean)
colnames(So_SN) <- c("So", "Mean_SN")

K_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$K), FUN = mean)
colnames(K_SN) <- c("K", "Mean_SN")

r_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$r), FUN = mean)
colnames(r_SN) <- c("r", "Mean_SN")

sigma_SN <- aggregate(data$SN_ratio, by = list(data$sigma), FUN = mean)
colnames(sigma_SN) <- c("sigma", "Mean_SN")

print(So_SN)
print(K_SN)
print(r_SN)
print(sigma_SN)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Plot main effects for each factor
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# So
plot(1:3, So_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "red",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Stock Price (So)", ylab = "S/N Ratio (dB)",
     main = "Main Effect - So (T = 3 Months)")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = So_SN$So)

# K
plot(1:3, K_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "green",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Strike Price (K)", ylab = "S/N Ratio (dB)",
     main = "Main Effect - K (T = 3 Months)")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = K_SN$K)

# r
plot(1:3, r_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "blue",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Interest Rate (r)", ylab = "S/N Ratio (dB)",
     main = "Main Effect - r (T = 3 Months)")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = r_SN$r)

# sigma
plot(1:3, sigma_SN$Mean_SN, type = "b", pch = 19, col = "purple",
     xaxt = "n", xlab = "Volatility (σ)", ylab = "S/N Ratio (dB)",
     main = "Main Effect - σ (T = 3 Months)")
axis(1, at = 1:3, labels = sigma_SN$sigma)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Identify best combination (highest mean S/N)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
best_So <- So_SN$So[which.max(So_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_K <- K_SN$K[which.max(K_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_r <- r_SN$r[which.max(r_SN$Mean_SN)]
best_sigma <- sigma_SN$sigma[which.max(sigma_SN$Mean_SN)]

cat("Best combination (T = 3 Months):\n")
cat("So =", best_So, "\n")
cat("K =", best_K, "\n")
cat("r =", best_r, "\n")
cat("sigma =", best_sigma, "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Interpretation
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\nInterpretation Notes:\n")
cat("- Higher S/N ratio → higher and more consistent BS value.\n")
cat("- Best combination = levels giving maximum S/N.\n")
cat("- Steeper slope in main effect plot → more influential factor.\n")







```
